---
title: "Partitioning Energy"
output: 
  html_document: 
    toc: true
    toc_float: true
    theme: cosmo
    css: style.css
---


```{r setup, include=FALSE}
knitr::opts_chunk$set(echo = TRUE)
```

\newcommand{\para}[1]{\left( #1 \right)}
\newcommand{\W}{\mathcal{W}}
\newcommand{\R}{\mathbb{R}}
\newcommand{\C}{\mathbb{C}}
\newcommand{\Z}{\mathbb{Z}}
\newcommand{\E}{\mathbb{E}}
\newcommand{\Cov}{\mathrm{Cov}}
\newcommand{\Var}{\mathrm{Var}}

## The Idea

* Individuals partition the energy they intake into either survival or reproduction. If it's all allocated to reproduction, they won't survive to reproduce and thus have a fitness of zero. Conversly, if it's all allocated to survival, they won't reproduce and thus still have a fitness of zero.

* So if $p$ is the propotion allocated one way or the other, then the population can only persist if there's individuals with $p\in(0,1)$. We can model the evolution of $p$.
